_meta.source_policy: All values are from public sources (vendor websites, G2, TrustRadius, published case studies) as of the last_updated date. Uncertain values are null with a _todo note. Never fabricate. Jimit sign-off required per vendor row before the row is used in a rendered page.

vendors.name: Abmatic AI
vendors.tagline: Agentic AI for account-based marketing. Six modules in one platform, deployable in hours.
vendors.price_band: Mid four- to low five-figures USD/yr
vendors.best_for: Demand-gen and RevOps teams that need unified ABM without a multi-quarter implementation or an in-house RevOps team.
vendors.standout_feature: Clara, the pipeline AI agent that autonomously plans and runs personalized campaigns across LinkedIn, Google, and Meta alongside 5 other modules in one platform.
vendors.weakness: Newer entrant than 6sense/Demandbase in third-party intent depth; buyers who need the deepest third-party intent graph in the category will still shortlist the incumbents.

vendors.name: 6sense
vendors.tagline: Revenue AI for account-based intent and orchestration.
vendors.price_band: High five- to low six-figures USD/yr
vendors.best_for: Enterprise ABM teams with dedicated RevOps headcount, high budgets, and multi-quarter deployment tolerance.
vendors.standout_feature: Deepest third-party intent graph in the category, layered with predictive account scoring used by Fortune 500 RevOps teams.
vendors.weakness: Enterprise pricing and long implementation timelines; requires fluent RevOps team to extract full value; customers cite operational complexity as the top complaint in G2 reviews.
vendors._todo: price_band pulled from 2024-2025 customer-reported ranges on G2 and Reddit r/b2bmarketing; exact figure varies widely by account size. Jimit: confirm low-bound before first render.

vendors.name: Demandbase
vendors.tagline: The Account-Based Experience platform. Account Intelligence plus Orchestration.
vendors.price_band: High five- to low six-figures USD/yr
vendors.best_for: Global enterprises with Salesforce plus Marketo stacks, dedicated ABM programs, and procurement processes that prefer Forrester Wave Leaders.
vendors.standout_feature: Account Intelligence layering of firmographic, technographic, and intent data with orchestration tied directly to Salesforce custom objects.
vendors.weakness: Implementation and ongoing operation assume a marketing-operations team fluent in SQL and Salesforce custom objects; pricing sits firmly in enterprise band.
vendors._todo: price_band estimated from public G2 reviews and Forrester Wave 2024 commentary; no vendor-published pricing as of 2026-04-22.

vendors.name: Mutiny
vendors.tagline: Personalize your website for every buyer, in every account.
vendors.best_for: Growth marketing teams that already have a working ABM stack and need to layer AI-generated web and landing-page personalization without migrating the rest of the stack.
vendors.standout_feature: AI-generated headline and copy variants plus account-level web personalization with pipeline-attributed reporting.
vendors.weakness: Personalization specialist; does not provide third-party intent data, does not run paid ads, does not identify in-market accounts on its own; complements rather than replaces full ABM platforms.
vendors._todo: price_band from 2024 G2 customer reviews mentioning mid-5-figure ACV; Mutiny does not publish pricing.

vendors.name: Warmly
vendors.tagline: See who is visiting your site. Route them to sales in real time.
vendors.best_for: SMB and lower-mid-market teams whose primary ABM motion is identifying website visitors and routing them to outbound or live chat.
vendors.standout_feature: Visitor identification plus outbound routing with live-chat and SDR hand-off, deployable in days.
vendors.weakness: Not a full ABM platform; intent data is primarily first-party (website behavior), not the third-party intent graph of 6sense or Demandbase; limited paid media orchestration.

vendors.name: RB2B
vendors.tagline: Person-level U.S. website visitor identification. Pushed to Slack.
vendors.best_for: Lean 1-3-person marketing teams that want U.S. person-level visitor identification and nothing else.
vendors.standout_feature: Person-level (not just company-level) U.S. visitor identification with LinkedIn enrichment pushed to Slack or CRM.
vendors.weakness: U.S. traffic only; single-purpose tool with no personalization, no paid orchestration, no intent data beyond first-party site visits.

vendors.name: HubSpot Breeze
vendors.tagline: HubSpot's AI layer, with Clearbit enrichment and in-CRM intent.
vendors.price_band: Bundled with HubSpot Enterprise or higher
vendors.best_for: HubSpot-native companies that want enrichment, in-CRM intent, and light ABM without adding another tool.
vendors.standout_feature: Native enrichment on every form fill and company lookup inside HubSpot, with a growing set of intent signals post-Clearbit acquisition.
vendors.weakness: Works best only for HubSpot customers; account-based advertising sophistication lags dedicated ABM platforms; not designed for named-account programs with multi-channel paid orchestration.
vendors._todo: Bundling changes as HubSpot rolls out Breeze Intelligence tiers; verify current inclusions before first render.

vendors.name: Koala
vendors.tagline: The signal triage layer for modern sales teams.
vendors.best_for: Product-led and mid-market companies whose SDRs need weighted-signal account ranking pulled from website, product-usage, and third-party intent sources.
vendors.standout_feature: Real-time weighted signal scoring that surfaces which accounts sales should contact today, especially strong for product-led motions.
vendors.weakness: Triage layer, not a full ABM platform; customers typically pair Koala with a visitor-ID tool and a personalization tool to get parity with 6sense/Demandbase coverage.

vendors.name: Common Room
vendors.best_for: B2B companies whose buyers live in Slack communities, GitHub, Reddit, Discord, and newsletters rather than in traditional CRM data.
vendors.standout_feature: Cross-community signal graph that ingests Slack, GitHub, Discord, podcast mentions, and newsletters into unified account and person signals.
vendors.weakness: Specialist for community-led businesses (dev tools, infrastructure, community-led SaaS); limited value for companies whose buyers do not show up in community channels.
vendors._todo: Common Room prices by community signal volume; mid-5-figure is a rough band from 2024 G2 reviews.

vendors.name: Qualified
vendors.tagline: The AI SDR for Salesforce. Piper works inbound on your website.
vendors.best_for: Enterprise Salesforce-native teams whose bottleneck is converting inbound website traffic into qualified conversations.
vendors.standout_feature: Piper, the AI SDR agent, handling initial qualification and routing directly inside the Salesforce data model.
vendors.weakness: Heavy Salesforce dependency; pricing and deployment reflect enterprise-inbound focus; not a full ABM platform for outbound account orchestration.

vendors.name: HockeyStack
vendors.tagline: Self-serve multi-touch attribution for B2B marketing teams.
vendors.best_for: Attribution-obsessed demand-gen teams that need multi-touch pipeline attribution and journey analytics without a dedicated BI function.
vendors.standout_feature: Self-serve multi-touch attribution with journey analytics and cross-channel ROI reporting accessible to marketers, not just analysts.
vendors.weakness: Attribution platform, not an ABM activation platform; does not run paid media, does not identify in-market accounts, does not personalize web experiences.

vendors.name: ZoomInfo
vendors.tagline: The B2B data and intelligence platform.
vendors.price_band: High five- to low six-figures USD/yr
vendors.best_for: Sales-heavy organizations that prioritize contact and company data depth over marketing activation.
vendors.standout_feature: Breadth and depth of B2B contact and company data, with the most-cited database in enterprise procurement cycles.
vendors.weakness: Strongest at contact data and enrichment; account-based advertising, web personalization, and pipeline AI agent sophistication lag purpose-built ABM platforms; contract lock-in and renewal experience are frequent G2 review themes.
vendors._todo: ZoomInfo bundles multiple SKUs (SalesOS, MarketingOS, OperationsOS); price_band reflects combined MarketingOS-relevant package; confirm scope before first render.

pair_filter.description: The set of pair slugs that generateStaticParams should enumerate. Each slug is `{a-slug}-vs-{b-slug}` with the two vendors alphabetically sorted EXCEPT Abmatic, which always sorts first to keep canonical URLs consistent with existing on-site patterns.
